dialogueChunks.intro: You did it. You actually did it. {player_name}, I don't have the words.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The whole settlement is talking about what you've done. {target_npc} is already distributing the {quest_item} and the relief on people's faces... I haven't seen smiles like that in weeks. You've given us a future.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this. It's everything we could scrape together. I know it's not much compared to what you risked, but please accept it with our deepest thanks.

playerResponses.text: Seeing everyone safe is reward enough.
You're welcome. Take better care of your supplies next time.

dialogueChunks.intro: You're alive. When you didn't return by nightfall I feared the worst.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We lost two homes in the flooding while you were gone, and old {target_npc} passed in the night. But the {quest_item} you brought back will see the rest of us through. It's not the ending any of us wanted, but it's an ending we can live with.
dialogueChunks.outro: Here. {target_npc} wanted you to have this. Said you'd earned it ten times over. I think they were right.

playerResponses.text: I wish I could have done more.
At least something good came of it.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}! Come, come, you have to see this!
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Look at {location}: the storehouse is full, the gardens are planted again, and {target_npc} has already started organizing repair crews. All because you answered when we called. This place was dying before you arrived, and now there's laughter in the square again.
dialogueChunks.outro: We put something aside for you. And know this: you will always have a home here, for as long as these walls stand.

playerResponses.text: That means more to me than any treasure.
It was nothing. Just glad {location} is back on its feet.
Keep building. I might need a place to rest between adventures.

dialogueChunks.intro: When I saw you coming over the hill, I broke down crying. I'm not ashamed to say it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {quest_item} is already being put to use. {target_npc} hasn't stopped working since you handed it over: mending, sorting, distributing. For the first time in weeks, people aren't whispering about leaving {location}. They're talking about staying. About rebuilding. About a future.
dialogueChunks.outro: We gathered what we could to repay you. It doesn't begin to cover what you've done, but take it with the weight of every grateful soul in this settlement behind it.

playerResponses.text: Seeing this place come back to life is payment enough.
You all did the hard part: you survived. I just carried a few things.

dialogueChunks.plotStep: I won't pretend everything is perfect. We buried two people this morning who held on as long as they could but couldn't hold on long enough. The {quest_item} saved the rest, though. {target_npc} says another day and we would have lost a dozen more. You cut it close, but you cut it in time. That's what matters.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this. And if you ever pass through {location} again, know that there are graves here that would have been a graveyard without you.

playerResponses.text: The ones who held on: they fought as hard as I did.

dialogueChunks.intro: There you are! I've been watching the road for hours. The children spotted you first, actually: they've been calling you 'the hero' all day.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} organized a feast. I know, I know: we can barely afford it. But they insisted, said morale matters as much as supplies. And they're right. The {quest_item} is stored safely, the repairs are underway, and for the first time since this nightmare started, people at {location} are laughing over dinner.
dialogueChunks.outro: Sit with us tonight if you can. And take this: it's the best we have, saved for whoever pulled us out of the dark.

playerResponses.text: A feast sounds wonderful. I'd be honored to stay.
Save the food for your people. I'll take the reward and move on.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. Before you say anything, just look around.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The well is flowing again. The storehouse has walls. {target_npc} even got the old windmill turning yesterday. None of it would exist without what you brought back. People who were packing their things a week ago are now unpacking. A family that had given up planted a garden this morning, {player_name}. A garden. That's what hope looks like.
dialogueChunks.outro: This isn't much compared to what you risked. But every person here contributed something to this bundle. It's from all of us.

playerResponses.text: A garden. That's the best reward I could ask for.
Glad to see {location} on the mend. Take care of each other.

dialogueChunks.intro: I almost didn't recognize you under all that grime. Come, sit down, you've earned a rest.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: It's quiet now. The good kind of quiet, I mean: not the silence of dread, but the calm that comes when the worst is over. {target_npc} distributed the last of the {quest_item} this morning and says we have enough to last the season. The {enemy_type} haven't been seen near {location} since you went through. I think you put a proper scare into them.
dialogueChunks.outro: Here. We set this aside before you even left, because I knew you'd come through. Call it faith, or call it desperation: either way, you proved it right.

playerResponses.text: Faith suits me better than desperation. Take care of yourselves.
The quiet is nice. I could get used to it.
